title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 8: Algebra and Geometry
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 2026 - 1998 + 72?
A rectangle has a length of 7 cm and a width of 5 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 80'><rect x='20' y='20' width='100' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='65' y='15' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>9 cm</text><text x='125' y='45' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Rectangle with length 9 cm and width 4 cm labelled.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2 x (7 + 5) = 2 x 12 = 24 cm.

questions.question_text: If 3x + 5 = 20, what is the value of x?
The pattern goes 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ... What is the 8th number in the pattern?
questions.solution: Each term increases by 3. The nth term is 2 + 3(n-1). For n=8: 2 + 21 = 23.

questions.question_text: How many small squares of side 1 fit inside a square of side 5?
questions.solution: A 5 x 5 grid contains 25 unit squares.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 14:35 and lasts 1 hour and 50 minutes. When does it end?
Which of the following numbers is divisible by both 4 and 6?
questions.solution: A number divisible by both 4 and 6 must be divisible by 12. Among the options, 24 = 12 x 2.

questions.question_text: Anna has 3 more stickers than Ben. Together they have 19 stickers. How many stickers does Ben have?
questions.solution: Let Ben have x. Then Anna has x + 3. x + (x+3) = 19, so 2x = 16, x = 8.

questions.question_text: In the diagram, which vertex shows a right angle (marked by the small square)?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 120'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><path d='M20 100 L60 40'/><path d='M60 40 L100 100'/><path d='M100 100 L140 40 L180 100'/><path d='M20 100 L180 100'/></g><text x='15' y='115' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>A</text><text x='58' y='35' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>B</text><text x='95' y='115' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>C</text><text x='138' y='35' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>D</text><text x='180' y='115' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>E</text><rect x='140' y='78' width='8' height='8' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Zigzag path with vertices A, B, C, D, E. A small square marker is shown near vertex D.
questions.solution: The small square marker indicates the right angle, which is at vertex D.

questions.question_text: What is half of 1/4?
The sum of three consecutive integers is 72. What is the largest of these integers?
questions.solution: Let the numbers be n-1, n, n+1. Their sum is 3n = 72, so n = 24. The largest is n+1 = 25.

questions.question_text: A square is divided into four equal rectangles by three parallel lines. If each small rectangle has a perimeter of 20 cm, what is the side length of the square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 140'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='100' height='100'/><line x1='45' y1='20' x2='45' y2='120'/><line x1='70' y1='20' x2='70' y2='120'/><line x1='95' y1='20' x2='95' y2='120'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square divided into four equal vertical rectangles.
questions.solution: Each small rectangle has dimensions s/4 by s. Perimeter = 2(s + s/4) = 5s/2 = 20, so s = 8.

questions.question_text: The ratio of boys to girls in a class is 3:4. If there are 21 boys, how many students are in the class in total?
questions.solution: If 3 parts = 21, then 1 part = 7, and girls = 4 x 7 = 28. Total = 21 + 28 = 49.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest positive integer that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by each of 3, 4, and 5?
questions.solution: The number minus 1 must be divisible by lcm(3,4,5) = 60. So the smallest is 60 + 1 = 61.

questions.question_text: How many different three-digit numbers can be formed using each of the digits 2, 5, and 7 exactly once?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 60'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='30' height='30'/></g><text x='20' y='37' font-size='18' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='60' y='37' font-size='18' fill='currentColor'>5</text><text x='100' y='37' font-size='18' fill='currentColor'>7</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three boxes containing the digits 2, 5, and 7.
questions.solution: With three distinct digits, the number of arrangements is 3! = 6.

questions.question_text: A triangle has angles measuring 40 degrees and 65 degrees. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 120'><polygon points='20,100 140,100 90,20' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='25' y='95' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>40</text><text x='120' y='95' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>65</text><text x='82' y='40' font-size='11' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Triangle with two angles labeled 40 degrees and 65 degrees, and the third angle marked with a question mark.
questions.solution: Angles in a triangle sum to 180 degrees. 180 - 40 - 65 = 75 degrees.

questions.question_text: In a family, each girl has at least two sisters and each boy has at least one brother. What is the smallest possible number of children in the family?
questions.solution: Each girl must have 2 sisters, so there are at least 3 girls. Each boy must have a brother, so there are at least 2 boys. Total at least 3 + 2 = 5 children.

questions.question_text: A bottle holds 1.5 liters of juice. How many milliliters remain after pouring out 650 ml?
Look at the sequence of shapes: triangle, square, circle, triangle, square, circle, ... What shape is in position 50?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,45 35,15 50,45'/><rect x='65' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><circle cx='125' cy='30' r='15'/><polygon points='150,45 165,15 180,45'/><rect x='195' y='15' width='20' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Repeating sequence of triangle, square, circle.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 positions. 50 divided by 3 leaves remainder 2, so position 50 is a square.

questions.question_text: Emma spent 2/5 of her money on a book and then 1/3 of what remained on a pen. She had $12 left. How much money did she start with?
questions.solution: After the book she had 3/5 left. After the pen she had 2/3 of that, i.e. (2/3)(3/5) = 2/5 of the original. So 2/5 of original = 12, giving 30.

questions.question_text: A rectangle of dimensions 8 by 6 has a small rectangle of dimensions 3 by 2 cut from one corner. What is the area of the remaining shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 140'><path d='M20 20 L140 20 L140 50 L110 50 L110 110 L20 110 Z' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='15' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>8</text><text x='145' y='35' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='120' y='80' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: L-shaped figure from an 8 by 6 rectangle with a 3 by 2 corner removed.
questions.solution: Original area = 8 x 6 = 48. Cut area = 3 x 2 = 6. Remaining = 48 - 6 = 42.

questions.question_text: Two numbers have a sum of 40 and a difference of 14. What is the larger number?
How many two-digit numbers have a digit sum equal to 9?
questions.solution: They are 18, 27, 36, 45, 54, 63, 72, 81, 90. That is 9 numbers.

questions.question_text: On a 4 by 4 grid, how many squares in total can be found (of all sizes)?

Four friends Alex, Bea, Cara, and Dan sit in a row of four seats numbered 1 to 4 from left to right. Cara is in seat 1. Alex is not in an end seat. Bea sits immediately to the right of Alex. Who sits in seat 4?
questions.solution: Cara = seat 1. Alex is in seat 2 or 3 (not at ends). If Alex = 2, Bea = 3, so Dan = 4. If Alex = 3, Bea = 4, so Dan = 2. Both arrangements satisfy all conditions, so seat 4 could be either Bea or Dan. The answer cannot be determined.

questions.question_text: A recipe uses flour and sugar in the ratio 5:2. To make a batch using 350 g of flour, how much sugar is needed?
questions.solution: 350 g corresponds to 5 parts, so 1 part = 70 g. Sugar = 2 x 70 = 140 g.

questions.question_text: A cube has a total surface area of 150 square cm. What is the length of one edge?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 140'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='30' y='40' width='70' height='70'/><path d='M30 40 L50 20 L120 20 L100 40'/><path d='M100 110 L120 90 L120 20'/></g></svg>
questions.solution: Surface area = 6 x edge^2 = 150, so edge^2 = 25, edge = 5 cm.

questions.question_text: A train travels 240 km in 3 hours. At the same constant speed, how far does it travel in 6 hours?
questions.solution: Speed = 240 / 3 = 80 km/h. Distance in 6 hours = 80 x 6 = 480 km.

questions.question_text: If a * b is defined as 2a + b, what is the value of (3 * 4) * 5?
A regular hexagon is divided into six equal triangles by its three main diagonals. If the area of the hexagon is 54 square cm, what is the area of one triangle?
questions.image_description: Regular hexagon divided into six equal triangles through the center.
questions.solution: Each triangle has area 54 / 6 = 9 square cm.
